These lines radiate diagonally from an implied point above, creating a sense of pattern and movement. The painting depicts the biblical scene of Jesus revealing the identity of Judas Iscariot at the Last Supper. Christ, centrally positioned, stands with long brown hair and a beard, wearing a dark blue robe with an outer robe in purple or grey hues. To his left stands Simon Peter, with a beard and two-toned hair. In front of Christ is a woman looking upwards in surprise. To the right lies Judas Iscariot, identifiable by his white head covering and draped garment. The figures show a range of expressions from surprise to sadness. The setting is an interior room, likely a dark chamber or cellar. A large rock formation behind the central figures serves as part of the natural setting.
